questions.questionText: The most common definitive sign of pregnancy on ultrasound at 5-6 weeks gestation is the presence of a:
questions.optionD: Gestational sac with a yolk sac
questions.explanation: The yolk sac confirms an intrauterine pregnancy before the embryo is clearly visible.

questions.questionText: A 28-year-old primigravida at 32 weeks presents with a blood pressure of 150/100 mmHg and +2 proteinuria. The most likely diagnosis is:
questions.explanation: New-onset hypertension and proteinuria after 20 weeks define preeclampsia.

questions.questionText: The definitive, life-saving treatment for severe preeclampsia/eclampsia is:
questions.optionA: Delivery of the fetus and placenta
questions.explanation: While MgSO4 prevents seizures and antihypertensives control BP, only delivery cures the condition.

questions.questionText: Which drug is the gold standard for the prevention and treatment of eclamptic seizures?
questions.explanation: MgSO4 is superior to other anticonvulsants for eclampsia.

questions.questionText: A pregnant woman presents with sudden, dark red vaginal bleeding and severe, constant abdominal pain. The uterus is woody hard and tender. Diagnosis?
questions.explanation: Premature separation of the placenta causes painful bleeding and a hypertonic uterus.

questions.questionText: Painless, bright red vaginal bleeding in the third trimester is the classic presentation of:
questions.explanation: The placenta implants over or near the internal cervical os, bleeding when the cervix dilates.

questions.questionText: The most common cause of postpartum hemorrhage (PPH) is:
questions.explanation: Failure of the uterus to contract ('boggy uterus') accounts for 70-80% of PPH.

questions.questionText: Which uterotonic agent is contraindicated in a patient with severe asthma and postpartum hemorrhage?
questions.explanation: Prostaglandin F2 alpha causes severe bronchoconstriction.

questions.questionText: A pregnant woman at 28 weeks has a 1-hour 50g oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT) result of 180 mg/dL. What is the next best step?
questions.optionC: Advise dietary modifications and retest in 4 weeks
questions.explanation: A failed 1-hour screen requires the definitive 3-hour diagnostic OGTT.

questions.questionText: The most common presentation of a twin gestation is:
questions.explanation: Both fetuses presenting vertex is the most common and safest for vaginal delivery.
